steps.title: Trace the causal chain
steps.content: The passage explains that baleen whales cycle iron in the ocean, which supports phytoplankton, which in turn sustain krill. That means whale populations control the base of the chain. Over the last century, baleen whale populations declined — so we need to follow that decline through the chain.

steps.title: Eliminate wrong choices and confirm the answer
steps.content:
Work through all four choices:
  • Choice A — The maximum body sizes of humpback and southern right whales have increased over the last century.: The passage gives body sizes as fixed examples, not as changing over time. Nothing supports body sizes increasing. ✗
  • Choice B — The quantity of krill needed to support a single southern right whale has increased over the last century.: The passage says whales eat a percentage of their body mass — there is no claim about the quantity of krill per whale changing. ✗
  • Choice C — The iron richness of the krill that baleen whales eat has declined over the last century.: The passage says whales cycle iron to support phytoplankton; it says nothing about the iron content of krill themselves. ✗
  • Choice D — Populations of phytoplankton that krill fed on have declined over the last century.: Fewer whales $\Rightarrow$ less iron cycling $\Rightarrow$ fewer phytoplankton. The passage explicitly says phytoplankton sustain krill, confirming phytoplankton declined when whale populations did. ✓
That matches Choice D — Populations of phytoplankton that krill fed on have declined over the last century..
